key_points: Dorm GroupMe culture is lively but chaotic
Campus described as beautiful by visiting and incoming students
No major housing complaints surfaced; limited direct data
key_quotes: I still remember the excitement I felt as a freshman years ago. It's a beautiful campus where I made lots of friends.
My freshman dorm GroupMe had a guy ask for a tampon to plug up his nose bleed

key_points: Greek life active; Kappa Sigma removed for hazing and alcohol
Bar strip (Murphy's, Outlaws, Willie's) was the main scene but is collapsing
Sports culture dominates weekends; hockey and wrestling have passionate fanbases
key_quotes: Murphy's and Outlaws were the two main spots on weekends.
Outlaws was the spot everyone went at the end of the night but honestly it has always sucked. It's overpriced and is nothing more than the front door to the Stillwater PD's checkpoint.
Kid died for like a minute [at Kappa Sigma].

key_points: Meal plan doesn't refund unused balances upon withdrawal
One student had $1,115 left with no refund option on withdrawal
No strong praise or consistent complaints about food quality in data
key_quotes: I have 1,115.44 left on my meal plan, what should I buy... I won't get a refund of it no matter what and I have to withdraw.
If anyone cared, my professor let me take the final again.

key_points: No direct data on counseling services or mental health support
Students dealing with family crises (e.g., homelessness) turned to Reddit, not OSU resources
High-stress football culture and safety incidents add campus anxiety
key_quotes: My mom has kicked me out (F17) and sadly I wasn't able to take my dog with me.
I need help.

key_points: Out-of-state cost runs ~$30k/year with no scholarship
National Merit Scholarship is a known pathway to major aid
OSU regularly cited as an affordable safety school for in-state students
key_quotes: Im an out of state freshman attending Oklahoma State University and the cost is around 30,000... I don't know how I can pay for everything with no scholarship.
OSU is the highest ranked midsized employer in Oklahoma — The lowest wages ever too.

key_points: Widely perceived as an easy-admission safety school nationally
Strong programs in agriculture, engineering, and computer science noted
Canvas LMS security breach interrupted finals with poor admin response
key_quotes: A safety is a school that will 100% admit you, guaranteed, based on your standardized test scores. An example would be Oklahoma State.
Lack of response shows admin is very much not prepared and also doesn't want to say anything so they're keeping quiet.
Lol isn't that the school where they fired a prof for failing a student because the student said the bullshit she wrote was protected by 'freedom of religion'?

key_points: Football program mismanagement cost taxpayers ~$100M over Gundy's tenure
Admin slow to respond to LMS security breach during finals
Opioid settlement foundation separated from OSU to avoid public reporting
key_quotes: After his $15 million buyout we (OK taxpayers) will have given him around $100 million in his 21 years as head coach.
Now that the foundation is no longer affiliated with the university, it will not be subject to the same public reporting requirements.
We're in full clean house mode 4 games into the season… awesome.

key_points: Stillwater is a small college town with limited infrastructure
Downtown small businesses struggling; fewer options for non-students
Big Orange Bus to Tulsa is a cheap and convenient transit option
key_quotes: Downtown Stillwater feels impact of still being downtown Stillwater.
Stillwater has a hockey team??? Wow I am so far behind the trends.
It's $15 for students on the Big Orange Bus. I would recommend it.

key_points: Alumni have reached notable positions including aerospace and tech sectors
OSU grads working for companies that helped return humans to the moon
Limited employer prestige outside Oklahoma; viewed as regional school nationally
key_quotes: OSU alumna and CEO of Frontier Electronics Brenda Rolls helped send mankind back to the moon.
A heroin addict gets clean and attains a Computer Information Systems degree with a 4.0 average.
OSU is the only higher education institution in Oklahoma on the Forbes highest-ranked midsized employer list.

key_points: In-state tuition is a strong value; out-of-state is a harder sell
National Merit Scholars can get exceptional deals here
Meal plan non-refund policy can cost students hundreds of dollars
key_quotes: I have a 13k a year [scholarship] to OSU making it semi-affordable, plus almost all of my friends are going there.
I won't get a refund of it no matter what and I have to withdraw.
Imagine getting paid $15m because you're so bad at your job they can't stand to be around you. Who says the American dream is dead?

key_points: Strong sports and community identity; fans are loyal even through losing seasons
Campus atmosphere is fun and welcoming but heavily tied to football culture
Non-country students worry about fitting in; veterans say it's not that extreme
key_quotes: We can lose everything but the party.
He's definitely not country. We are from Texas, but he's very mainstream — golf, basketball, etc. Any personal experiences?
Best school ever.

key_points: **Campus Safety:** Convicted serial rapist Jesse Butler received no jail time; community outrage ongoing
**Administration:** $15M football buyout while athletic and academic admin accountability questioned publicly
**Greek Life:** Kappa Sigma removed after hazing incident where a student nearly died
key_quotes: Jesse Mack Butler has pleaded guilty to multiple counts of sexual assault involving several girls but will not serve time in jail.
This entire case exposes how money and power still run Stillwater and Oklahoma.
Kid died for like a minute [during Kappa Sigma hazing].

key_points: Club hockey team is thriving with a winning record and passionate fanbase
Wrestling program is elite; GIA packed for matches, national championship pedigree
Sailing club at Lake Carl Blackwell is low-cost and open to all
key_quotes: I'll take being a Hockey School over the embarrassment of a football program we have any day.
A packed GIA is a wonderful sight for sore eyes!
OSU has some of the lowest membership dues among the various sports clubs.
